issues.title: CopilotPanel chat input and messages lack ARIA roles and live region announcements
issues.description: The chat panel has no role='log' on the message container, no aria-live='polite' for new messages, no aria-label on the input or send button. Screen readers cannot navigate the conversation. PAGE_ACCEPTANCE_CRITERIA Global Standards require all pages pass axe-core with zero critical/serious violations.
issues.prompt: In the file src/components/ai/CopilotPanel.tsx, in the CopilotPanel component: find the scrollable messages container div and add role='log' and aria-live='polite' and aria-label='AI conversation messages'. Find the AgentMentionInput or textarea element wrapper and ensure it has aria-label='Message to AI copilot'. Find any send button and add aria-label='Send message'. Find the close button (X icon) and ensure it has aria-label='Close AI copilot panel'. BEFORE: The messages container has no ARIA roles; the input has no aria-label; buttons rely solely on icons without accessible names. AFTER: Messages container has role='log' aria-live='polite' aria-label='AI conversation messages'; input has aria-label='Message to AI copilot'; close button has aria-label='Close AI copilot panel'; send button has aria-label='Send message'. After changes, run: npm run build to verify no TypeScript errors.

issues.title: FloatingAIButton silently fails when useAIInsights returns error with no user-visible retry path
issues.description: When useAIInsights fails (isError=true), the button shows a small AlertTriangle icon but clicking it calls refetch() instead of openCopilot(). Users see a tiny warning triangle with no text explanation. The title attribute says 'Click to retry' but a retry of a background query is not a useful action. Per acceptance criteria, network failures need toast notification + inline retry button, not a silent icon swap.
issues.prompt: In the file src/components/ai/FloatingAIButton.tsx, in the FloatingAIButton component: BEFORE: When isError is true, the onClick handler calls refetch() and the title says 'AI insights unavailable. Click to retry.' with only a tiny AlertTriangle badge. AFTER: When isError is true, the onClick handler should first call refetch() and then also call openCopilot() so the user gets the copilot panel open. Change the error badge to include a tooltip-style title 'AI insights could not load. Click to open copilot and retry.' Also, add an aria-label that matches the title text for screen reader accessibility. The button should still function as the copilot opener even during error state, not become a retry-only button. After changes, run: npm run build to verify no TypeScript errors.

issues.title: CopilotPanel has no responsive layout — fixed 480px width clips on mobile viewports
issues.description: The CopilotPanel slides in from the right at a fixed width (likely 480px based on acceptance criteria description). On mobile viewports (375px iPhone SE), this panel either overflows or is inaccessible. Per PAGE_ACCEPTANCE_CRITERIA Global Standards, every page must be usable at 375px width. The panel should be full-screen on mobile with bottom sheet behavior.
issues.prompt: In the file src/components/ai/CopilotPanel.tsx, in the CopilotPanel component: find the main panel container div that has position fixed and a width style. BEFORE: The panel has a fixed width (e.g., 480px or similar) that does not adapt to mobile viewports. AFTER: Change the width to use a responsive approach: width should be 'min(480px, 100vw)' so on mobile it fills the screen. Add a media query check or use window.innerWidth to detect mobile (under 768px) and if mobile, set width to '100vw', remove any right offset, and ensure the panel covers the full screen. Also ensure the input area at the bottom has padding-bottom of at least 'env(safe-area-inset-bottom)' for iPhone notch/home bar. After changes, run: npm run build to verify no TypeScript errors.

issues.title: AgentMentionInput textarea has no character limit enforcement despite MAX_MESSAGE_LENGTH constant
issues.description: The file defines MAX_MESSAGE_LENGTH = 4000 but never enforces it. The handleChange callback does not check input length, and there is no visual character counter. Users can paste arbitrarily long text which will fail at the API layer with no helpful error. Per acceptance criteria, forms should validate on blur with clear error states.
issues.prompt: In the file src/components/ai/AgentMentionInput.tsx, in the AgentMentionInput component: find the handleChange callback and the textarea element. BEFORE: MAX_MESSAGE_LENGTH is declared as 4000 but never used. The textarea accepts unlimited input. There is no character counter visible to the user. AFTER: In handleChange, add a check: if text.length > MAX_MESSAGE_LENGTH, truncate to MAX_MESSAGE_LENGTH and call toast.error('Message is too long. Maximum 4000 characters.') once (use a ref to prevent repeated toasts). Add maxLength={MAX_MESSAGE_LENGTH} to the textarea element. Below the textarea, add a small character counter div showing `${value.length}/${MAX_MESSAGE_LENGTH}` styled with fontSize caption, color textTertiary, and change color to statusCritical when value.length > MAX_MESSAGE_LENGTH * 0.9. After changes, run: npm run build to verify no TypeScript errors.

issues.title: AI insights endpoint returns generic starter fallbacks with no real project data context
issues.description: When both aiService and Supabase cache return empty, getAiInsights returns hardcoded fallback insights with generic titles like 'Schedule Analysis Available'. These are not real insights — they are placeholders. Per acceptance criteria, dashboard metric cards must show real calculated numbers, not placeholders. The fallback insights have no project-specific data, making the AI panel feel fake.
issues.prompt: In the file src/api/endpoints/ai.ts, in the getAiInsights function: find the section where cachedInsights.length === 0 && budgetInsights.length === 0 triggers starterInsights creation. BEFORE: The fallback creates generic insights like 'Schedule Analysis Available' with hardcoded IDs and no project context. These display as real AI insights but contain no actual analysis. AFTER: Change the fallback insights to be clearly marked as onboarding prompts, not analysis results. Set their type to 'onboarding' (add this as a valid type if needed), set severity to 'info', and change titles to actionable prompts: 'Connect your schedule data to enable AI delay predictions', 'Add budget line items to unlock cost variance analysis', 'Create daily logs to power AI field summaries'. Set confidence to 0 (instead of a misleading high value) and add a field `isPlaceholder: true` to the AIInsight objects so the UI can render them differently (e.g., with a dashed border instead of solid). After changes, run: npm run build to verify no TypeScript errors.

competitive_intel: Procore's Procore Assist is a conversational AI that searches specs, RFIs, submittals, and building codes — it answers questions from real indexed project documents. SiteSync's AI Copilot has a richer multi-agent architecture with specialist agents (schedule, cost, safety) and generative UI rendering, which is architecturally ahead. However, the current implementation falls short because the AI insights fall back to generic placeholders when no real data exists, the copilot panel lacks mobile responsiveness for field use, and accessibility is missing entirely — a superintendent using VoiceOver on an iPhone cannot interact with the copilot at all. Procore Agent Builder's no-code AI agents are a competitive threat; SiteSync's @mention routing to specialist agents is a strong differentiator if it works reliably with real project data.
summary: The Project Intelligence module (Dashboard AI + Copilot) has an ambitious and well-architected multi-agent system with generative UI components, specialist agent routing via @mentions, and batch action previews. However, it suffers from five critical gaps in SURGEON scope: (1) zero ARIA accessibility on the copilot panel makes it unusable for screen readers, violating global acceptance criteria; (2) the FloatingAIButton error state traps users in a retry loop instead of opening the copilot; (3) the copilot panel has no mobile responsive layout, making it unusable on the 375px viewports superintendents use in the field; (4) the AgentMentionInput declares but never enforces its 4000 character limit; and (5) AI insights fall back to generic placeholders that masquerade as real analysis when no data exists. Fixing these five issues would move the module from 59 to approximately 72, primarily by making the copilot actually usable on mobile, accessible to all users, and honest about when it has real data versus placeholder content.
